symptom: Pattern memory not growing
root_cause: See report
minimal_fix: Fixed feature data conversion in pattern pipeline
verification: Patterns now recording
Memory growing

symptom: Pattern memory was not growing despite hours of trading. The bot only showed the BASE_PATTERN despite processing hundreds of trades.
root_cause:
The pattern recording pipeline lost features data when converting from pattern objects to the recordPatternResult API:

1. **analyzePatterns()** creates patterns with `features` field (numeric array)
2. **recordPatternResult()** was called with `signature` field (truncated string)
3. **recordPattern()** expects features array, not signature string
4. Validation failed on signature string, patterns never stored
minimal_fix: Fixed feature data conversion in pattern pipeline
verification: Patterns now recording
Memory growing

symptom: - Three different sources of "truth" tracking the same data:
root_cause:
- Three different sources of "truth" tracking the same data:
  - `this.currentPosition` in main bot (run-empire-v2.js)
  - `this.tradingBrain.position` in OptimizedTradingBrain
  - `this.executionLayer.positions` Map in AdvancedExecutionLayer
- Each module updating its own copy → phantom trades, balance mismatches
- "3 truths = 0 truth" - complete state chaos
minimal_fix: - **File**: `run-empire-v2.js` (multiple locations)
verification: StateManager is now the ONLY source of truth
No more phantom trades or balance mismatches
All modules read/write to same centralized state
what_worked: StateManager is now the ONLY source of truth
No more phantom trades or balance mismatches
All modules read/write to same centralized state

symptom: - TRAI (AI decision system) blocked main loop for 2-5 seconds per decision
root_cause:
- TRAI (AI decision system) blocked main loop for 2-5 seconds per decision
- During volatile moves, bot was blind while waiting for AI to "think"
- Flash crashes could happen while bot waited for philosophical analysis
minimal_fix: - **File**: `run-empire-v2.js` lines 931-954
verification: Bot never waits for TRAI decisions
Can react instantly to market moves
TRAI does post-trade learning only
what_worked: Bot never waits for TRAI decisions
Can react instantly to market moves

symptom: - StateManager used Maps for activeTrades
root_cause:
- StateManager used Maps for activeTrades
- JavaScript Maps can't be serialized to JSON
- On restart, all active trades were lost
- Bot had amnesia after every restart
minimal_fix: - **File**: `core/StateManager.js` lines 326-385
verification: Active trades persist across restarts
Bot remembers exact position after crash
No more lost trades on reboot
what_worked: Bot remembers exact position after crash
No more lost trades on reboot
Automatic save after every state update

symptom: - kraken_adapter_simple.js works but doesn't implement IBrokerAdapter interface
root_cause:
- kraken_adapter_simple.js works but doesn't implement IBrokerAdapter interface
- System expects v2 architecture with 30+ standard methods
- "Running a Ferrari off a matchbox car engine"
minimal_fix: - **File**: `core/KrakenAdapterV2.js` (NEW FILE - 322 lines)
verification: Full IBrokerAdapter compliance
Position tracking via StateManager
Account polling (compensates for no private WebSocket)
what_worked: Account polling (compensates for no private WebSocket)
Working solution now, native rewrite later

symptom: - Rate limiter used recursion for retries
root_cause:
- Rate limiter used recursion for retries
- On 429 errors, created infinite promise chain
- Stack overflow after ~1000 rate limit hits
- Memory leak from accumulated promises
minimal_fix: - **File**: `kraken_adapter_simple.js` lines 109-204
verification: No more stack overflow on rate limits
No more promise accumulation/memory leak
Clean queue-based retry mechanism
what_worked: No more stack overflow on rate limits

symptom: Bot buying on bearish/hold brain signals - ~50% of positions opened against brain direction
root_cause: makeTradeDecision() line 1908 only checked `pos === 0 && totalConfidence >= minConfidence` without verifying brainDirection === 'buy'. Brain could say 'sell' or 'hold' and bot would still open a long position.
minimal_fix: Added `&& brainDirection === 'buy'` to BUY condition at run-empire-v2.js:1908
verification: Backtest pending - expect ~50% fewer trades but much higher win rate

symptom: MaxProfitManager tiered profit exits completely dead - exit_partial action silently ignored
root_cause: run-empire-v2.js:2082 only checked `profitResult.action === 'exit' || profitResult.action === 'exit_full'`. MaxProfitManager returns 'exit_partial' for tier hits (line 440) - this action was silently dropped.
minimal_fix: Added `|| profitResult.action === 'exit_partial'` to the exit check at run-empire-v2.js:2082. Also passes exitSize in return for partial position closing.
verification: Backtest pending - expect tiered exits to fire on profitable positions

symptom: Fees never deducted from balance - P&L overstated by ~108%, backtest results unreliable
root_cause: StateManager.openPosition() and closePosition() moved USD in/out without deducting Kraken trading fees (0.26% per side). All P&L calculations were fee-blind.
minimal_fix: Added 0.26% fee deduction on both entry (openPosition line 316) and exit (closePosition line 400) in StateManager.js
verification: Backtest pending - expect lower raw P&L but accurate real-world numbers

symptom: holdTime calculations used Date.now() instead of candle timestamps - all hold times ~0 in backtest, time-based exits broken
root_cause: 6 locations in run-empire-v2.js used Date.now() for entryTime recording and holdTime/holdDuration calculations. In backtest mode, Date.now() is wall clock time not simulated candle time, so hold durations were microseconds instead of minutes. Time-based exits (30min stale trade, min hold time) were completely inverted.
minimal_fix: Replaced Date.now() with `this.marketData?.timestamp || Date.now()` at lines 2090, 2134, 2320, 2432, 2522, 2528. Passed candle timestamp via context to StateManager.openPosition().
verification: Backtest pending - expect correct holdTime values, 30min stale exit to fire properly

symptom: Breakeven stop triggered guaranteed losses - fee buffer 0.1% vs actual 0.32% round-trip fees
root_cause: MaxProfitManager.js:730 set feeBuffer = 0.001 (0.1%). Kraken round-trip fees are 0.52% (0.26% x 2 sides). 'Breakeven' stop was actually locking in a -0.22% loss every time it triggered.
minimal_fix: Changed feeBuffer from 0.001 to 0.0035 at MaxProfitManager.js:730
verification: Backtest pending - expect breakeven stops to actually break even

symptom: Default exit thresholds (SL=1.5%, TP=2.0%, Tiers 0.5%/0.8%/1.2%) causing bleeds in ranging markets
root_cause: Stop loss too tight (1.5%) getting clipped before price could breathe. Profit tiers too aggressive, cutting winners short.
minimal_fix: Config D validated: STOP_LOSS_PERCENT=2.0, TAKE_PROFIT_PERCENT=2.5, TIER1_TARGET=0.007, TIER2_TARGET=0.010, TIER3_TARGET=0.015
verification: 6 configs tested across 3 market segments (uptrend, range, chop)
Config D: +0.05% avg vs Config A baseline -0.07%
Wider 2.0% SL lets trades breathe, tighter tiers cut winners
what_failed: Config F (tighter tiers 0.4%/0.6%/0.8%) - cut winners short

symptom: Low confidence trades bleeding fees. Signal generator had implicit 40% floor, testing 25-60% thresholds.
root_cause: MIN_TRADE_CONFIDENCE=25% let garbage signals through. 60% starved trades in uptrends.
minimal_fix: MIN_TRADE_CONFIDENCE=0.50 (50%) - filters garbage without starving good signals
verification: Tested 25%, 30%, 40%, 50%, 60% thresholds
50%: 41 trades, +0.09% avg (winner)
60%: 23 trades, +0.01% avg (starved)
25%: 57 trades, +0.05% avg (too many garbage)
what_worked: 50% threshold killed seg_5 bleed while keeping uptrend trades
what_failed: 30% and 40% identical to 25% due to signal generator floor at 40%

symptom: Need to confirm tuned config doesn't overfit to training data
root_cause: Config tuned on 2024 segments could be overfit. Needed out-of-sample validation.
minimal_fix: Validated on 2023 data (unseen) and full 45K dataset
verification: Out-of-sample 2023: 4000 candles, $16.5K→$41.5K, +0.05%, 23 trades, 69.6% WR
Full 45K: +0.73%, 289 trades, 63.7% WR, 0.92% max DD
Config generalized across regimes - not overfit

symptom: Production bot backtest shows confidence=0 on ALL signals, brainDirection=hold, zero trades executing
root_cause: StrategyOrchestrator has 9 strategies but most need specific conditions (EMA crossovers, S/R bounces, liquidity sweeps). In trending data where price stays above MAs, no strategy fires. minStrategyConfidence=0.35 filters everything.
minimal_fix: PENDING - Options: 1) Add simpler momentum strategy 2) Lower threshold for backtest 3) Use tuning-backtest.js results instead
verification: Tested with seg_16_uptrend: EMA sig=neutral, MA sig=neutral, orchestrator returns 0 strategies
what_worked: tuning-backtest.js works because it uses simpler RSI-based logic
what_failed: Production orchestrator too selective for backtest data
evidence: backtest-full-tuned.txt shows confidence:0 on all TRADE_DECISION logs

symptom: Pattern bank shows 8181 patterns when we deleted them. Two different pattern files exist.
root_cause: Two pattern storage locations: 1) trai_brain/learned_patterns.paper.json (4 patterns, clean) 2) data/pattern-memory.paper.json (8181 patterns, bloated). Bot loads from data/ not trai_brain/.
minimal_fix: Delete data/pattern-memory.paper.json and backup files
verification: rm data/pattern-memory.paper.json data/pattern-memory.paper.backup-*.json
evidence: ls -la shows both files exist with different sizes

symptom: Bot crash loop after restart - Cannot find module kraken_adapter_simple, TierFeatureFlags, OgzTpoIntegration
root_cause: Files incorrectly archived as dead code. Static analysis missed dynamic dependencies via ModuleAutoLoader.get() and IBroker wrapper chain. Bot ran on stale memory for 16h.
minimal_fix: Restored files from archive: kraken_adapter_simple.js (root), TierFeatureFlags.js (root), OgzTpoIntegration.js (core/)
verification: Bot starts without module errors
lesson: NEVER archive without Bombardier dynamic dependency check. IBroker wrappers hide actual implementations. ModuleAutoLoader.get() is invisible to static analysis.

symptom: PAPER_TRADING=true in .env but bot sent real orders to Kraken, resulting in $50 loss
root_cause: OrderExecutor.js only checked BACKTEST_MODE, ignored PAPER_TRADING flag entirely. Orders went to real exchange despite paper mode being enabled.
minimal_fix: Added PAPER_TRADING constant and check in OrderExecutor.js line 24-25 and 105-106
verification: Bot shows 'PAPER MODE: Simulating order' when PAPER_TRADING=true
No real orders sent to exchange
what_worked: Adding explicit PAPER_TRADING check alongside BACKTEST_MODE
what_failed: Trusting that flag existed without verifying code enforced it
evidence: PM2 logs showed real Kraken orders despite PAPER_TRADING=true
lesson: ALWAYS trace flags through to enforcement point. Existence != enforcement.

symptom: MAX_DRAWDOWN and MAX_DAILY_LOSS flags loaded but never enforced. Trading continued even when limits exceeded.
root_cause: RiskManager had isTradingAllowed() and assessTradeRisk() methods but TradingLoop never called them. Methods existed but were dead code.
minimal_fix: Added riskManager.isTradingAllowed() and assessTradeRisk() checks before BUY decisions in TradingLoop.js lines 421-462
verification: Log shows 'RISK BLOCK: [reason] - Trade rejected' when limits exceeded
what_worked: Wiring existing enforcement code to decision path
evidence: grep showed riskManager methods never called in hot path
lesson: Flag audit: trace from .env to enforcement point. If method exists but nobody calls it, flag is cosmetic.

symptom: Position size could exceed MAX_POSITION_SIZE when confidence multiplier was high
root_cause: Confidence multiplier (up to 2.5x) was applied after reading MAX_POSITION_SIZE but no cap was enforced afterward. 10% max * 2.5x = 25% actual.
minimal_fix: Added cap in OrderExecutor.js: if (basePositionPercent > maxPositionPercent * 2.5) basePositionPercent = maxPositionPercent * 2.5
verification: Log shows 'Position capped: X% → Y% (MAX_POSITION_SIZE limit)' when exceeded
evidence: Code review showed no bounds check after multiplier
lesson: Always enforce caps AFTER all multipliers are applied.

symptom: Confidence always 0% after restart. RSI=null despite having 16 candles in priceHistory.
root_cause: priceHistory loaded 97 candles from disk but IndicatorEngine started empty. Out of sync - priceHistory.length=16 but IndicatorEngine.candles.length=3.
minimal_fix: Added indicatorEngine.computeBatch(priceHistory) in run-empire-v2.js lines 602-610 to replay saved candles through indicators on startup
verification: Log shows 'IndicatorEngine synced with priceHistory (RSI: 68.3)' on startup
Confidence > 0% immediately after restart
what_worked: computeBatch() designed exactly for this - deterministic replay
evidence: Debug trace showed IndicatorEngine had 3 candles while priceHistory had 16
lesson: State that loads from disk must be replayed through any stateful calculators (indicators, EMAs, etc).

symptom: EMASMACrossover and MADynamicSR strategies never fire after restart
rootCause: Signal modules are stateful (crossoverState, prevSpreads, swings, srLevels) but started empty while priceHistory loaded 103 candles from disk
fix: Replay saved candles through emaCrossover.update() and maDynamicSR.update() on startup in constructor
prevention: Any stateful module that depends on historical data must replay saved candles on startup

symptom: MADynamicSR detected 0 swings even with 200+ candles replayed
rootCause: minBars check (220 candles) blocked execution BEFORE _detectSwings() was called. Swing detection requires only 7 candles but was gated by EMA requirement.
fix: Move _detectSwings() and _updateSRLevels() to start of update() BEFORE minBars check so swings accumulate during warmup
prevention: Separate warmup requirements: swing detection (7 candles) vs EMA calculation (220 candles)

symptom: LiquiditySweep constructor was empty {}, ignoring all 12 TradingConfig params
root_cause: Constructor used wrong key names vs what TradingConfig provided. Zero params were being read.
minimal_fix: Wire all 12 params from TradingConfig.get('strategies.LiquiditySweep') in run-empire-v2.js lines 516-528
verification: Constructor now reads sweepLookbackBars=50, entryWindowBars=18, etc.
what_worked: Direct TradingConfig wiring matching production pattern
lesson: Always verify constructor params match TradingConfig keys exactly.

symptom: MADynamicSR using 20 MA as S/R level (wrong interpretation of Trader DNA method)
root_cause: Misinterpretation of Trader DNA method - 20 MA is for slope/trend detection, 200 MA is for S/R bounces
minimal_fix: Full rewrite: 20 MA for slope trend detection only, 200 MA for S/R bounce entries. Added proper slope calculation, extension detection, first-touch skip.
verification: Strategy now fires on 200 MA bounces with 20 MA slope confirmation
what_worked: Proper separation of trend detection (20 MA) vs S/R (200 MA)
what_failed: Original conflation of both into one signal
lesson: Read the source material (Trader DNA) before implementing strategy logic.

symptom: MAExtensionFilter in orchestrator duplicated MADynamicSR's internal extension detection - stacked gates killing signals
root_cause: 6 filters between signal and trade, MAExtensionFilter was redundant after MADynamicSR rewrite
minimal_fix: Disabled MAExtensionFilter in StrategyOrchestrator.js lines 115-136
verification: Signals now reach decision layer without redundant extension check
what_worked: Disabling redundant gate, letting strategy handle its own filtering
lesson: Stacked gates compound rejection rates. Remove redundant filters when strategy handles detection internally.

symptom: VP chop filter blocking trend strategies in balanced markets - another stacked gate
root_cause: VolumeProfile regime filter was one of 6 gates between signal and trade. Strategies already handle their own slope/chop detection.
minimal_fix: Disabled VP chop filter in StrategyOrchestrator.js lines 391-412
verification: RSI/EMA signals no longer blocked by external regime filter
what_worked: Letting strategies handle own regime detection
lesson: Centralized regime filters + per-strategy regime filters = signal death. Pick one.

symptom: OrderExecutor had hardcoded 0.32% fees instead of TradingConfig values. AdaptiveTimeframeSelector had 0.26% hardcoded.
root_cause: Fee values scattered across codebase instead of single source of truth in TradingConfig
minimal_fix: OrderExecutor: TradingConfig.get('fees.makerFee'), TradingConfig.get('fees.takerFee'). AdaptiveTimeframeSelector: same pattern. Removed dead config from TradingConfig.
verification: All fee calculations now from TradingConfig
lesson: Every magic number should trace back to TradingConfig or .env.

symptom: Backtest constructors empty {} while production used full TradingConfig. Every backtest number was fantasy.
root_cause: tuning-backtest-full.js never updated when production constructors changed. EMACrossover, LiquiditySweep, VolumeProfile all empty.
minimal_fix: Sync all constructors with run-empire-v2.js: EMACrossover (3 params), MADynamicSR (12 params), LiquiditySweep (10 params), VolumeProfile (5 params)
verification: Backtest output now shows same config as production
what_worked: Copy exact constructor pattern from run-empire-v2.js
lesson: Backtest MUST mirror production exactly. Any constructor change needs to propagate to tuning scripts.

symptom: FEES_PCT defaulted to 0.25% (one side) not 0.50% (round-trip). All P&L was optimistic.
root_cause: Default value only accounted for maker fee, not round-trip (maker + taker)
minimal_fix: Changed FEES_PCT default from 0.25 to 0.50 in tuning-backtest-full.js line 45
verification: Backtest header shows 'Fees/slippage: 0.5% per trade'
lesson: Always use round-trip fees for P&L calculations. Entry + exit.

symptom: 5 files still had hardcoded fee values (0.0026, 0.0035, 0.0052) scattered through codebase
root_cause: Historical fixes applied piecemeal - never centralized to TradingConfig. BacktestRecorder, PnLCalculator, MaxProfitManager, StateManager all had hardcoded values.
minimal_fix: Replace all hardcoded fees with TradingConfig.get() calls. Added TradingConfig imports where needed.
verification: grep -r "0.0026\|0.0035\|0.0052" core/ returns 0 matches
Smoke test passes (13/13)
what_worked: Adding TradingConfig imports to files that lacked them
lesson: Fee values must NEVER be hardcoded. All magic numbers trace to TradingConfig.

symptom: LiquiditySweep feedCandle() expected 1m candles but received 15m from production. Opening candle collected 225min instead of 15min. Entry window 15x too long.
root_cause: Internal 1m→15m aggregation assumed 1m input, but production websocket sends 15m candles directly. Aggregation multiplied timing by 15x.
minimal_fix: Removed aggregation buffers (_minuteBuffer5m, _minuteBuffer15m). Process 15m candles directly. Session open detection checks if 15m candle contains open minute.
verification: Backtest trades increased 21→159 (7.6x)
LiquiditySweep alone: 96 trades
Smoke test passed
what_worked: Pipeline STRUCTURAL fix with replacement blocks
what_failed: Internal aggregation assumed wrong input timeframe
lesson: Always verify input timeframe matches expected. If production sends 15m, don't aggregate 1m→15m internally.

symptom: LiquiditySweep blocked all signals during ATR warmup - 0 trades for first ~15 days
root_cause: Line 215 required dailyATR to be non-null. Lines 221-222 produced NaN threshold when dailyATR was null, causing isManipCandle=false.
minimal_fix: L215: Remove dailyATR check. L221: Ternary for null. L222: Default true when threshold null.

symptom: TradingConfig sent entryWindowBars but detector reads entryWindowMinutes - config value silently ignored
root_cause: Timeframe-agnostic rewrite changed config key from bars to minutes but TradingConfig not updated
minimal_fix: Changed 4 files: TradingConfig, run-empire-v2, tuning-backtest-full, smoke-test to use entryWindowMinutes:90

symptom: RiskManager reading process.env.RISK_MANAGER_BYPASS directly at runtime
root_cause: Legacy pattern - process.env accessed in module instead of injected via constructor
minimal_fix: Add riskManagerBypass to constructor config, pass from resolvedConfig in run-empire-v2.js
verification: Runtime env reads: 76 → 74
Syntax check passed
Bot startup verified
lesson: All config should flow through ConfigLoader → constructor injection, never direct process.env access.

symptom: Two separate pattern stores causing data fragmentation - PatternMemorySystem in EnhancedPatternRecognition and PatternMemoryStore for TRAI
root_cause: Historical organic growth created two separate pattern stores with different APIs and storage formats. TRAI couldn't read patterns learned by the pipeline.
minimal_fix: Created UnifiedPatternMemory singleton with DTW matching. Wired to EnhancedPatternRecognition, TRAIDecisionModule, trai_core, OrderExecutor. Deleted old stores.
verification: TRAI logs show DTW matches with ~97% similarity
4276 patterns migrated successfully
Bot startup clean, no errors
what_worked: Singleton pattern for single source of truth
DTW fuzzy matching finds similar patterns
Compatibility layer for old API calls
lesson: Pattern stores should be unified from day one. DTW matching better than exact hash for 9-element feature vectors.

symptom: TRAI model unloaded from VRAM between 15-minute trade cycles, causing slow first inference each cycle
root_cause: Ollama default keep_alive is 5 minutes, but trade interval is 15 minutes. Model unloaded during idle time.
minimal_fix: Added keep_alive: '20m' to Ollama generate requests in persistent_llm_client.js
verification: Model stays loaded in VRAM with 20m expiry
Each TRAI call resets timer
lesson: Match keep_alive to trade interval + buffer. 20m for 15m interval works.

symptom: RSI null DTO validation errors spamming logs during indicator warmup
root_cause: IndicatorSnapshotDTO required RSI to be a number, but RSI is null during first 14 candles of warmup
minimal_fix: Made RSI nullable in schema: z.number().min(0).max(100).nullable()
verification: Clean logs during warmup
No DTO errors on startup
lesson: Indicator values can be null during warmup - DTOs should account for this.

symptom: TRAI warmup returned empty response, causing fallback warning
root_cause: Warmup used 10 max tokens but reasoning models need room for <think> blocks before producing output
minimal_fix: Increased warmup tokens from 10 to 100 in persistent_llm_client.js
verification: Warmup completes with valid response
No empty response warnings
what_worked: Giving reasoning model room to think
lesson: Reasoning models (deepseek-r1, qwen3) need 50-100+ tokens even for simple prompts due to internal <think> blocks.

symptom: TRAI system prompt was crypto-only, couldn't advise on stocks
root_cause: Modelfile.trai had 'cryptocurrency trading AI' in system prompt
minimal_fix: Expanded system prompt to cover stocks, ETFs, and crypto with technical analysis capabilities
verification: TRAI responds correctly to stock queries (TSLA, SPY, etc.)
Technical indicator analysis works for all asset types
what_worked: Broader system prompt covering all tradeable assets
lesson: System prompts should match the full scope of intended use cases.

symptom: CandlePatternDetector.js existed with 12+ pattern types but was NEVER IMPORTED - orphan code generating no signals
root_cause: CandlePatternDetector was built but never wired into TradingLoop. Only EnhancedPatternRecognition.analyzePatterns() was called, which only queries pattern memory (not detect real patterns).
minimal_fix: Import CandlePatternDetector, call detect() alongside analyzePatterns(), merge results
verification: Real candle patterns (hammer, engulfing, doji) now generate signals

symptom: TradingConfig had per-timeframe SL/TP/trail settings but ExitContractManager never read them - all trades used 15m defaults
root_cause: createExitContract() only read DEFAULT_CONTRACTS per strategy, ignoring timeframeConfig with proper 1m/5m/15m/1h/4h parameters.
minimal_fix: Add timeframe parameter to createExitContract(), call TradingConfig.getTimeframeConfig(timeframe)
verification: 1m trades: 0.5% SL, 4h trades: 3.5% SL

symptom: minTradeConfidence=0.01 (1%) let everything pass - rubber stamp gate causing massive churn (15,633 trades)
root_cause: Default 0.01 was set to 'allow env var overrides' but in practice meant no filtering at all.
minimal_fix: Raised minTradeConfidence from 0.01 to 0.35 (35%)
verification: Trades now require 35% minimum confidence

symptom: IndicatorEngine returns superTrendDirection but downstream expects indicators.trend - always undefined, RegimeDetector gets bad data
root_cause: Data structure mismatch between IndicatorEngine DTO output and TradingLoop expectations.
minimal_fix: Add backward compat mapping: indicators.trend = indicators.superTrendDirection || 'sideways'
verification: RegimeDetector and pattern analysis receive proper trend data

symptom: Multi-position fix allowed stacking 50 longs on same candle - 15,633 trades in 2yr backtest instead of ~300
root_cause: TradingLoop checked activeTrades.length < maxPositions but not if same-direction position already existed.
minimal_fix: Add hasLongPosition/hasShortPosition checks with sameDirectionBlock gate before entry
verification: 1 long at a time, 1 short at a time, flipping allowed
